description_long: This is a roughly triangular, irregularly shaped stone with a pale beige to light gray coloration. One face features a shallow, carved decorative motif resembling a stylized leaf or scroll, suggesting it may have once been part of an architectural element such as a frieze or cornice. The other sides are unadorned, with a natural, weathered texture and subtle surface pitting. The stone appears solid and dense, with no visible cracks or major chips, and its surface is matte with a slightly rough tactile quality. The overall form is asymmetrical, with one broad, flat base and a gently tapering top. Its size and mass suggest it could serve as a substantial obstacle or boundary marker in an urban environment.
description: A pale, irregular stone with a carved decorative motif on one face and rough, unadorned surfaces on the others. The stone is solid, dense, and matte, with a slightly rough texture.
description_view_0: This view highlights the carved decorative motif, showing a stylized leaf or scroll pattern on the stone's surface.
description_view_1: This view shows the broad, unadorned face of the stone, revealing its natural, weathered texture and irregular shape.
description_view_2: This angle displays the stone's side, emphasizing its asymmetrical, blocky form and rough, pitted surface.
description_view_3: This view presents another angled side, showing the tapering top and the continuation of the stone's rough, matte finish.
attribute_location: historic urban park or architectural ruin
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or remnant near pathways or open plazas
attribute_pattern: shallow carved motif resembling a stylized leaf or scroll on one face; otherwise, natural pitting and subtle mottling
attribute_weathering: mildly weathered with a matte, slightly rough surface and minor pitting, indicating moderate exposure to the elements
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: contrasts with modern urban materials but may blend with other historic stonework or landscaping elements
